warps.note: Berry House entrance on western side of route, lower section
Mr. Pokemon's House entrance — upper northeast section of route

notes: 20 tiles wide x 54 tiles tall (10 blocks x 27 blocks)
Route runs north-south. Player enters from south (Cherrygrove) and travels north (Route 31).
Mr. Pokemon's House is in the upper-right area — warp at tile (17,5), sign at (15,5).
Fruit tree 1: tile (5,39). Fruit tree 2: tile (11,5).
Item ball (Antidote): tile (8,35). Hidden item (Potion): tile (14,9).
Surfable water runs along a stream on the east side of the route (mid section).
Youngster Joey: tile (2,28) — facing right, line of sight 3. EVENT_ROUTE_30_YOUNGSTER_JOEY.
Youngster battle script object (ROUTE30_YOUNGSTER1): tile (5,26) — EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE.
Monster sprites for Rattata battle setup: tiles (5,24) and (5,25). EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE.
Youngster Mikey: tile (5,23) — facing down, line of sight 1.
Bug Catcher Don: tile (1,7) — facing down, line of sight 3.
Non-trainer Youngster (Route30YoungsterScript): tile (7,30) — walk_left_right, gives Mr. Pokemon directions.
CooltrainerF (non-trainer NPC): tile (2,13) — facing down, warns about eye contact.

ROUTE30_YOUNGSTER4_SCRIPT_NPC.note: Non-trainer youngster giving directions to Mr. Pokemon

ROUTE30_MONSTER1.note: Rattata sprite, part of Joey's battle scene

ROUTE30_MONSTER2.note: Rattata sprite, part of battle scene, EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE

npcs.name: Youngster
npcs.dialogue_summary: Before player delivers Mystery Egg to Elm: gives directions to Mr. Pokemon's house ('It's a bit farther ahead'). After: comments that everyone is having fun battling.

npcs.name: CooltrainerF
npcs.dialogue_summary: Says she is not a trainer, but warns that making eye contact with a trainer means you have to battle.

trainers.name: Joey
trainers.rematch_note: Joey rematches via Pokegear phone. Team upgrades through JOEY1-JOEY5 based on game progress flags (Goldenrod flypoint, Olivine flypoint, Radio Tower, Elite Four). Can give HP_UP after defeating JOEY5.

dialogue.seen: I just lost, so I'm trying to find more Pokemon. Wait! You look weak! Come on, let's battle!
dialogue.beaten: Ack! I lost again! Doggone it!
dialogue.after: Do I have to have more Pokemon in order to battle better? No! I'm sticking with this one no matter what!

scene_event.description: Before battling Joey, a cutscene plays: Joey commands his Rattata to use Tackle on Mikey's Rattata. Dialogue then triggers the actual trainer battle. The Rattata sprites (ROUTE30_MONSTER1 at tile 5,25 and ROUTE30_MONSTER2 at tile 5,24) animate the tackle sequence.

dialogue.seen: You're a Pokemon trainer, right? Then you have to battle!
dialogue.after: Becoming a good trainer is really tough. I'm going to battle other people to get better.

dialogue.seen: Instead of a bug Pokemon, I found a trainer!
dialogue.after: I ran out of Poke Balls while I was catching Pokemon. I should've bought some more...

items.name: Antidote
items.note: Item ball on eastern side of route, lower-mid section

items.name: Potion
items.note: Hidden item (BGEVENT_ITEM). Check near trees in the upper section.

signs.text: TRAINER TIPS: No stealing other people's Pokemon! Poke Balls are to be thrown only at wild Pokemon!

fruit_trees.note: Berry fruit tree 1 — gives one berry per day. Lower-west section of route.
Berry fruit tree 2 — gives one berry per day. Upper section near Mr. Pokemon's House.

grass.note: 10% encounter rate each time period, from johto_grass.asm def_grass_wildmons ROUTE_30. Gold version has Caterpie; Silver version has Weedle in same slots.

water.note: From johto_water.asm def_water_wildmons ROUTE_30. 2% encounter rate.

fishing.note: Route 30 uses the Pond fish group (Poliwag-based). Data from fish.asm .Pond_* tables.

events.description: Visiting Mr. Pokemon triggers the scene in MrPokemonsHouse — not on the route itself but this is the route's primary story objective. Player receives Mystery Egg from Mr. Pokemon and Pokedex from Prof. Oak.

Special cutscene before battling Youngster Joey. Joey commands his Rattata to tackle Mikey's Rattata. The two MONSTER sprite objects (Rattata) animate the tackle using applymovement. This event clears once the battle has occurred. Controlled by EVENT_ROUTE_30_BATTLE flag.

notes: Route 30 is the second route in the game and the player's primary goal after getting their starter from Elm is to traverse it north to reach Mr. Pokemon's House. The route runs north-south and is notably narrow (10 blocks wide). There is surfable water mid-route on the east side. Three trainers are present: Bug Catcher Don guards the northern upper section near Mr. Pokemon's House, while Youngster Joey and Mikey are in the mid-lower section. Joey is the game's most famous recurring trainer — he can be rematched via Pokegear phone throughout the game with progressively stronger teams. The Berry House (Route30BerryHouse) at tile (7,39) is a side building not covered in this file but shares the route landmark.
